meta_title: For Freelancers: portfolio building to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to portfolio building and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Building Portfolios to Fix Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to build a high-converting portfolio using Antigravity and Claude Code to automate debugging and scale your freelance career.
content.answer_block: When you can't debug code, you aren't just stuck; you're losing billable hours. The solution is shifting from manual patching to systematic portfolio building. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural oversight and Claude Code for iterative execution, you transform debugging from a bottleneck into a repeatable, high-value asset for your freelance business.
content.intro:
The screen glows, the cursor blinks, and the error log is an endless sea of red. You’ve been chasing a memory leak for three hours, and your client’s deadline is looming like a storm front. This isn't just a bug; it's a productivity death spiral that separates the amateurs from the high-earning architects. 

But what if you stopped debugging one-off errors and started building a systemic portfolio of reusable code modules? By integrating AI-native workflows into your stack, you move from reactive patch-work to proactive engineering. It is time to stop fighting the machine and start orchestrating it to build your legacy.

steps.title: Define the Architectural Blueprint
steps.description: Before touching code, map out the project structure. This prevents 'spaghetti code' that becomes impossible to debug later.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project requirements document and generate a modular, scalable folder structure that isolates concerns, making future debugging trivial.

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Debug-proof your code by ensuring every function has a corresponding test suite before implementation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a comprehensive test suite for the authentication module that covers edge cases like null tokens and expired sessions.

steps.title: Deploy Defensive Error Handling
steps.description: Implement global error boundaries so the application fails gracefully instead of crashing during production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a global error handler middleware that logs stack traces to an external service and returns user-friendly messages.

steps.title: Refactor Legacy Blocks into Libraries
steps.description: Turn your debugged code into reusable assets for your portfolio, saving you hours on future client projects.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this utility function into a standalone, documentation-rich NPM package format for my freelance component library.

steps.title: Verify with AI-Driven Linting
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a final audit, catching logical errors that static linters often miss.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the current codebase for security vulnerabilities and performance bottlenecks. Suggest 3 optimizations.

pro_tips: Never fix the same bug twice; if it happens twice, turn the solution into a documented module.
Use Claude Code's 'review' flag during PRs to force documentation generation before merging.
Treat your portfolio like a product; the better the code quality, the higher the premium you can charge clients.
Always maintain a 'debugging journal' in a Markdown file to train your local context for future AI prompts.
Use Antigravity to simulate 'what-if' scenarios on your architecture to catch potential bugs before they reach runtime.
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, document your new modules in a central GitHub repository and begin offering 'Architectural Refactoring' as a high-ticket service to your existing clients.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting bugs dictate your income. Transform your technical debt into a scalable portfolio that sells itself. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
